华盛顿（路透社） -  Facebook周三向联邦法院向美国联邦贸易委员会提出的主要反垄断案件驳回，几乎所有美国国家，称他们未能展示该公司拥有垄断或伤害的消费者。
  “通过一次投票保证金，在造成的对抗宗教信仰与反垄断问题上的事项的无情批评的环境中，原子能机构决定对Facebook带来案件，”Facebook表示回应FTC投诉。
 “这里没有涉嫌反垄断行动的危害没有任何危害，”它说。
 在12月份提交的诉讼中，FTC和各国要求法院迫使社交媒体巨头销售两款珍贵资产，其消息传递应用程序WhatsApp和照片共享应用程序Instagram。美国区法官哥伦比亚地区詹姆斯Boasberg将听到案件。
 FTC和各国指示Facebook打破反托拉斯法，将较小的竞争对手保持在海湾，并将社交媒体竞争对手捕获，如2012年10亿美元的Instagram，2014年的WhatsApp为190亿美元。
  总而言之，联邦政府和国家在去年举行Facebook和Alphabet的谷歌提出了五个诉讼，继两党愤怒过度使用和滥用社会媒体群体在经济和政治领域。
 在对FTC诉讼的回应中，Facebook认为，政府未能表明Facebook在一个明确界定的市场中垄断，或者它伤害了消费者。 
该公司还驳回了Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg和其他高管担心Instagram和WhatsApp所带来的竞争威胁的其他高管们所讨论的电子邮件。
 “缺乏对消费者建立非法行为或伤害的事实，FTC试图用来自Facebook高管的抓取袋的抓取袋子抓住其索赔，这些电子邮件和来自Facebook高管的消息，这些信息被提出表明Facebook感到担心instagram的竞争威胁和WhatsApp  - 还有许多其他公司，“Facebook在其回应中表示。
  另外，在诉讼中带来了几十个国家和地区，Facebook认为应该被驳回国家案件，因为这些国家未能表明他们被Facebook伤害，因为他们等了四年以上。
 纽约律师佩特蒂亚詹姆斯表示，Facebook在法律上是错误的，对我们的投诉错误。“
 “我们对我们的案件充满信心，这就是为什么这个国家几乎每个国家都加入了我们的双层诉讼，以结束Facebook的非法行为，”她在一份声明中说。
note_en:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) - Facebook asked a federal court on Wednesday to dismiss major antitrust cases filed by the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and nearly every U.S. state, saying they failed to show the company had a monopoly or harmed consumers.
  “By a one-vote margin, in the fraught environment of relentless criticism of Facebook for matters entirely unrelated to antitrust concerns, the agency decided to bring a case against Facebook,” Facebook said in responding to the FTC complaint.
 “None of the harms typically alleged in antitrust actions is alleged here,” it said.
 In lawsuits filed in December, the FTC and states asked the court to force the social media giant to sell two prized assets, its messaging app WhatsApp and photo-sharing app Instagram. U.S. District Judge James Boasberg in the District of Columbia will hear the cases.
 The FTC and states accused Facebook of breaking antitrust law to keep smaller competitors at bay and snapping up social media rivals, like Instagram for $1 billion in 2012 and WhatsApp in 2014 for $19 billion.
  All told, the federal government and states filed five lawsuits against Facebook and Alphabet’s Google last year following bipartisan outrage over use and misuse of social media clout both in the economy and in the political sphere.
 In its response to the FTC lawsuit, Facebook argued that the government failed to show that Facebook had a monopoly in a clearly defined market or that it had hurt consumers.
 The company also dismissed emails cited in the FTC lawsuit written by Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg and other executives expressing worry about the competitive threat posed by Instagram and WhatsApp.
 “Lacking facts to establish either unlawful conduct or harm to consumers, the FTC attempts to bolster its claims with a grab-bag of selectively quoted internal emails and messages from Facebook executives, which are offered to show that Facebook was concerned about competitive threats from Instagram and WhatsApp – but also many, many other firms,” Facebook said in its response.
  Separately, in the lawsuit brought by dozens of states and territories, Facebook argued that the state case should be dismissed because the states failed to show that they were harmed by Facebook and because they waited more than four years.
 New York Attorney General Letitia James said that Facebook was “wrong on the law and wrong on our complaint.”
 “We are confident in our case, which is why almost every state in this nation has joined our bipartisan lawsuit to end Facebook’s illegal conduct,” she said in a statement.
title: Facebook要求法院解雇美国政府，国家反托拉斯案件
title_en: Facebook Asks Court To Dismiss US Government, States Antitrust Cases

柏林，3月10日（路透社） - 德国的多达60,000台计算机系统接触到一个缺陷，允许未经授权的用户在Microsoft Corp的电子邮件中访问系统，Watchdog在周三表示。
 BSI首席Arne Schoenbohm表示，在上周末向上周末进行了一系列警告，仍然在上周末发出警告后解决了超过一半的漏洞，但仍需要修复大约25,000个系统。
 “警告已奏效。在德国，许多交换服务器通过下载补丁来保护，“Schoenbohm在给路透社的书面评论中表示。 “每个弱势系统都是太多并且可能导致伤害。”
 缺陷似乎已被黑客广泛利用，影响了20,000多个美国组织。欧盟银行监管机构和挪威议会也受到了击中。
  在关于Microsoft漏洞的一个14页报告中，BSI表示，自从公开透露后，黑客利用它已经改变的黑客行为。
 最初，大多数目标都是思维坦克，大学，非政府组织，律师事务所和国防公司 - 主要在美国。
 报告称，现在，这些漏洞正在以大规模的规模部署在大规模范围内 - 显然是全球范围内的。“ 
根据网络安全公司ESET的研究人员，至少有10个不同的黑客组织使用Microsoft邮件服务器软件中的最新漏洞闯入全球目标。  在德国，BSI说，两名联邦当局受到了破解的影响，拒绝了这一点。  该BSI表示自周末以来大约100家公司从小企业到寻求指导的领先公司，远高于通常的数字。  “我们与所有计算机紧急响应团队（CERT）联系在国内外，特别是美国网络安全和基础设施安全局（CISA），”BSI表示，添加它也与微软密切联系。 （Raphael Satter的其他报告，由Douglas Busvine写作;通过Bernadette Baum编辑）
note_en:
BERLIN, March 10 (Reuters) - As many as 60,000 computer systems in Germany were exposed to a flaw that allows unauthorized users to access systems in Microsoft Corp’s email software, the head of its cybersecurity watchdog said on Wednesday.
 More than half of the vulnerabilities were addressed following a warning last weekend by the Federal Office for Information Security (BSI), but around 25,000 systems still need to be fixed, BSI chief Arne Schoenbohm said.
 “The warning has worked. In Germany, many Exchange servers have been secured by downloading patches,” Schoenbohm said in written comments to Reuters. “Every vulnerable system is one too many and can lead to harm.”
 The flaw appears to have been widely exploited by hackers and affected more than 20,000 U.S. organisations. The European Union’s banking regulator and the Norwegian parliament have also been hit.
  In a 14-page report on the Microsoft vulnerability, the BSI said the behaviour of hackers exploiting it had changed sharply since it was publicly revealed.
 Initially, most targets had been think tanks, universities, non-governmental organisations, law firms and defence companies - mostly in the United States.
 “Now, these exploits are being deployed at mass scale against thousands of targets - apparently worldwide,” the report said.
 At least 10 different hacking groups were using the latest flaw in Microsoft’s mail server software to break into targets around the world, according to researchers at cybersecurity company ESET.
  In Germany, two federal authorities have been affected by the hack, the BSI said, declining to say which.
 The BSI said it had been contacted since the weekend by around 100 companies ranging from small businesses to leading companies seeking guidance, well above the usual number.
 “We are in touch with all Computer Emergency Response Teams (CERT) in Europa and abroad, especially the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) in the United States,” the BSI said, adding it was also in close contact with Microsoft. (Additional reporting by Raphael Satter, Writing by Douglas Busvine; Editing by Bernadette Baum)
title: 在德国暴露在德国最多60,000台计算机系统到微软漏洞
title_en: Up To 60,000 Computer Systems Exposed In Germany To Microsoft Flaw

HTC在Twitter帐户上的最新挑逗与其Vive耳机的唇部跟踪模块相关。
 昨天，该公司下降了第二笔推进，这次与“表达自己”的“表达”不那么微妙的消息。有些粉丝通过附接到Vive Pro耳机的唇形跟踪模块的图像回复了推文。将设备连接到耳机底部的剪辑看起来近乎相同。
   与上周的挑逗结合在一起，包括“这是你的移动”，加上许多使用“跟踪”这个词甚至更新的参考的众多回复，它肯定似乎是HTC为其现有的Vive耳机缩短了新的加载项，而不是全新设备。昨天我们报告说，HTC在中国的vive总裁Alvin Wang Graying曾证实HTC将在2021年发布新的独立耳机，但现在似乎不太可能在这里戏弄的可能性。
 HTC揭示了唇部跟踪模块，以便在GDC 2019年举动。当该公司表示，它没有计划全面发布设备。该套件挂在耳机下方并读出嘴巴的运动，然后可以使用给定的经验来用来复制自己的虚拟化身。与HTC Vive Pro Eye中的眼睛跟踪配对，HTC很快就可以将非常富有表现力的虚拟化身带到VR。进一步逐步，实现vive跟踪器以进行全身跟踪，并且这里有很多潜力。
note_en:
HTC’s latest tease on its  Twitter account looks related to the lip-tracking module for its Vive headsets.
 Yesterday the company dropped a second teaser tweet, this time with the not-so-subtle message “Express yourself.” Some fans replied to the tweet with an image of the lip tracking module attached to the Vive Pro headset. The clip that attaches the device to the bottom of the headset looks near-identical.
   Paired with  last week’s tease that included the message “It’s your move”, plus numerous replies that use the word ‘tracking’ and even a more recent  reference to Avatar, it certainly seems like HTC is teasing new add-ons for its existing Vive headsets, rather than an all-new device.  Yesterday we reported that HTC’s President of Vive in China, Alvin Wang Graylin, had confirmed HTC will release a new standalone headset in 2021, but it now seems less likely that’s what’s being teased here.
 HTC revealed the lip-tracking module for Vive all the way back at GDC 2019. At the time the company said it had no plans for a full release of the device. The kit hangs below the headset and reads movement in your mouth, which a given experience could then use to replicate on a virtual avatar of yourself. Paired with the eye-tracking in the HTC Vive Pro Eye, it’s feasible that HTC could soon be bringing very expressive virtual avatars to VR. Go a step further and implement the Vive Trackers for full-body tracking, and there’s a lot of potential here.
title: HTC现在似乎是戏弄唇部跟踪
title_en: HTC Now Seems To Be Teasing Vive Lip-Tracking
